questions.text: What is the primary function of the Quran according to the opening of this Surah?
questions.answer: As guidance and glad tidings for the believers who establish prayer, give Zakat, and have certainty in the Hereafter.

questions.text: What happens to those who do not believe in the Hereafter according to this Surah?
questions.answer: Allah makes their deeds seem fair to them, so they wander blindly; they will have the worst punishment and will be the greatest losers.

questions.text: Briefly describe the 'fire' Musa (AS) saw in the direction of Tur.
questions.answer: He saw a fire and told his family to stay while he went to bring a brand or find guidance; instead, he was called by Allah from the blessed spot.

questions.text: What were the first two miracles given to Musa (AS) at the burning bush in this Surah?
questions.answer: His staff turning into a moving snake and his hand coming out white without any disease.

questions.text: How many total signs was Musa (AS) given to show Pharaoh and his people?
questions.answer: Nine signs.

questions.text: What was the specific blessing did Allah give Dawud and Sulayman that they were grateful for?
questions.answer: Knowledge, and they said, 'Praise to Allah, who has favored us over many of His believing servants.'

questions.text: Which Prophet inherited from his father and was taught the 'language of birds'?
questions.answer: Prophet Sulayman (AS) inherited from Dawud (AS).

questions.text: What were the three components of Sulayman's (AS) army?
questions.answer: Jinn, men, and birds.

questions.text: What did the female ant say when she saw Sulayman's army approaching?
questions.answer: 'O ants, enter your dwellings lest Sulayman and his hosts crush you while they perceive not.'

questions.text: What was Sulayman's (AS) reaction to the ant's warning?
questions.answer: He smiled, laughing at her speech, and prayed to Allah to enable him to be grateful for His favors and to do righteous deeds.

questions.text: Which bird was missing when Sulayman (AS) inspected the birds?
questions.answer: The Hoopoe (Hud-hud).

questions.text: What news did the Hoopoe bring back from Saba (Sheba)?
questions.answer: He found a woman (Bilqis) ruling over them with a magnificent throne, but they were worshipping the sun instead of Allah.

questions.text: How did the Hoopoe describe the role of Shaitan in Saba?
questions.answer: Shaitan had made their deeds seem fair to them and averted them from the way of Allah, so they were not guided.

questions.text: What was the content of the letter Sulayman (AS) sent to the Queen of Saba?
questions.answer: It started with 'Bismillah-ir-Rahman-ir-Rahim' and said: 'Be not haughty with me but come to me in submission (Muslims).'

questions.text: What was the advice of the Queen of Saba's chiefs regarding Sulayman's letter?
questions.answer: They said they were people of power and great military might, but the command was hers to decide.

questions.text: Why did the Queen of Saba decide to send a gift instead of declaring war?
questions.answer: She said that when kings enter a city, they ruin it and humiliate its noble people; she wanted to see how the messengers would return.

questions.text: How did Sulayman (AS) respond to the gift of wealth sent by the Queen?
questions.answer: He rejected it, stating that what Allah had given him was better, and he threatened to bring armies they could not resist.

questions.text: Who brought the throne of the Queen of Saba to Sulayman in the 'twinkling of an eye'?
questions.answer: One who had knowledge of the Scripture (Al-Kitab).

questions.text: What did Sulayman (AS) say when he saw the throne settled before him?
questions.answer: 'This is from the favor of my Lord to test me whether I will be grateful or ungrateful.'

questions.text: Why did Sulayman (AS) order for the throne to be disguised or altered?
questions.answer: To see if the Queen would recognize it or if she would be of those who are not guided.

questions.text: Describe the palace floor that amazed the Queen of Saba.
questions.answer: It was a courtyard (Sarh) of smooth glass; she thought it was a pool of water and uncovered her shins, but Sulayman told her it was just glass.

questions.text: What was the final realization of the Queen of Saba after seeing Sulayman's signs?
questions.answer: She said, 'My Lord, indeed I have wronged myself, and I submit with Sulayman to Allah, Lord of the worlds.'

questions.text: Which Prophet was sent to the people of Thamud in this Surah?
questions.answer: Prophet Salih (AS), who told them to worship Allah, but they became two parties quarreling.

questions.text: What did the nine men in the city of Prophet Salih plot to do?
questions.answer: They took a mutual oath to attack Salih and his family by night and then tell his protector they did not witness their destruction.

questions.text: How did Allah respond to the plot against Salih (AS)?
questions.answer: Allah planned a plan while they perceived not, and destroyed them and their people utterly.

questions.text: What was the specific message of Prophet Lut (AS) to his people in this Surah?
questions.answer: He asked, 'Do you commit immorality while you see? Do you approach men with desire instead of women?'

questions.text: What was the only response of Lut's people to his message?
questions.answer: They said, 'Expel the family of Lut from your city; indeed, they are people who keep themselves pure.'

questions.text: Which member of Lut's family was not saved from the punishment?
questions.answer: His wife; she was decreed to be among those who remained behind.

questions.text: How many times does the question 'Is there a god with Allah?' appear in the middle of the Surah?
questions.answer: Five times, following proofs of Allah's creation, provision, and guidance.

questions.text: Who responds to the distressed (Mudtarr) when he calls upon Him and removes evil?
questions.answer: Allah alone.

questions.text: Who guides people through the darkness of land and sea and sends winds as glad tidings?
questions.answer: Allah.

questions.text: Who begins creation and then repeats it, and provides from the heaven and the earth?
questions.answer: Allah.

questions.text: Does anyone in the heavens and the earth know the 'Ghayb' (unseen) besides Allah?
questions.answer: No, only Allah knows it, and they do not perceive when they will be resurrected.

questions.text: What is the 'clear Book' that mentions the things the Children of Israel differ about?
questions.answer: The Quran, which is a guidance and mercy for the believers.

questions.text: Can the Prophet make the 'dead' or the 'deaf' hear his message?
questions.answer: No, and he cannot guide the blind from their error; he can only make those hear who believe in Allah's verses.

questions.text: What is the 'Dabbat al-Ard' (Beast of the Earth) mentioned at the end of the Surah?
questions.answer: A sign of the Day of Judgment that will emerge and speak to people because they did not have certainty in Allah's signs.

questions.text: What will happen when the groups from every nation who denied the signs are gathered?
questions.answer: They will be set in rows and asked why they denied the signs without encompassing them in knowledge.

questions.text: What will happen when the Trumpet is blown at the end of time?
questions.answer: Everyone in the heavens and earth will be terrified, except whom Allah wills, and all will come to Him in humility.

questions.text: How are the mountains described in their movement on the Last Day?
questions.answer: They appear firm, but they will pass away like the passing of clouds—the work of Allah who perfected all things.

questions.text: What is the reward for one who brings a 'Hasanah' (good deed) on that Day?
questions.answer: He will have better than it, and they will be safe from the terror of that Day.

questions.text: What happens to those who bring a 'Sayyi'ah' (evil deed)?
questions.answer: Their faces will be cast into the Fire, and they will be asked, 'Are you recompensed except for what you used to do?'

questions.text: What was the Prophet (SAW) commanded regarding the 'Lord of this City' (Makkah)?
questions.answer: To worship the Lord of this city, who made it sacred and to whom belongs all things.

questions.text: What is the final command to the Prophet regarding the recitation of the Quran?
questions.answer: To recite the Quran; whoever is guided is guided for himself, and whoever strays, the Prophet is only a warner.

questions.text: What will Allah show people in the future so they will recognize them?
questions.answer: He will show them His signs, and your Lord is not unaware of what you do.

questions.text: What is the meaning of the name 'An-Naml'?
questions.answer: The Ants.

questions.text: What is the 'Mantiq al-Tayr'?
questions.answer: The language or speech of birds.

questions.text: What is the 'Hud-hud' bird?
questions.answer: The Hoopoe, a bird known for its role in the story of Sulayman and the Queen of Saba.

questions.text: What is 'Saba' (Sheba)?
questions.answer: An ancient kingdom in Yemen ruled by a Queen during the time of Sulayman.

questions.text: What is the 'Throne' (Arsh) of the Queen of Saba described as?
questions.answer: A 'Great Throne' (Arshun Azim), signifying her power and wealth.

questions.text: What is the 'Knowledge of the Scripture'?
questions.answer: A special wisdom or miracle-working power given by Allah to certain servants.

questions.text: What is the 'Glass Palace' (Sarh)?
questions.answer: A magnificent structure built by Sulayman to show the Queen of Saba the superior signs of his Lord.

questions.text: What is the 'Blessed Spot' (Al-Buq'ah al-Mubarakah)?
questions.answer: The area where Musa spoke to Allah near Mount Sinai.

questions.text: What are the 'nine signs' of Musa?
questions.answer: Miracles like the staff, the hand, the flood, locusts, lice, frogs, blood, years of famine, and shortage of fruits.

questions.text: Why did the Queen of Saba's people worship the sun?
questions.answer: Due to Shaitan's deception and following the traditions of their forefathers.

questions.text: What is the 'Clear sign' (A-ilahun ma'allah) meant to challenge?
questions.answer: The illogical belief in multiple gods when all signs of creation point to One.

questions.text: What is the 'Ghayb'?
questions.answer: The Unseen, including the future, the Hereafter, and the hidden secrets of the universe.

questions.text: What is the 'Certainty' (Yaqin) mentioned for the believers?
questions.answer: Unwavering faith in the truth of Allah and the Hereafter.

questions.text: What is the 'Trumpet' (Sur)?
questions.answer: The horn that will be blown by an angel to signal the end of the world and the resurrection.

questions.text: What is the 'Terror' (Faza') of the Last Day?
questions.answer: The overwhelming fear that will grip all creation when the world ends.

questions.text: What is the 'City' (Al-Baldah) that Allah made sacred?
questions.answer: Makkah.

questions.text: What is the 'Beast of the Earth' a sign of?
questions.answer: The closeness of the Day of Judgment when faith will no longer be accepted.

questions.text: What is the 'Inheritance' (Waritha) of Sulayman?
questions.answer: He inherited the prophethood, wisdom, and kingdom from his father Dawud.

questions.text: Why did Sulayman laugh at the ant's speech?
questions.answer: In wonder at her intelligence and in gratitude for being able to understand her.

questions.text: What is the 'Great Bounty' (Fadl) mentioned in the story of Dawud and Sulayman?
questions.answer: The unique gifts of knowledge and power bestowed upon them by Allah.

questions.text: What is the 'Guidance and Mercy' (Hudan wa Rahmah)?
questions.answer: The primary qualities of the Quran for those who choose to believe.

questions.text: What is the 'Lord of the worlds' (Rabb al-Alamin)?
questions.answer: The One who sustains and governs all existence.

questions.text: What is the 'submission' (Islam) Bilqis entered into?
questions.answer: Accepting the oneness of Allah and following His guidance.

questions.text: What is the 'Test' (Fitnah) mentioned regarding the two parties of Salih's people?
questions.answer: Their differing reactions to the truth—one group believing and the other denying.

questions.text: What is the 'Evil Rain' (Matar al-Saw')?
questions.answer: The rain of stones that destroyed the people of Lut.

questions.text: What is the 'Visible knowledge' (Ilm)?
questions.answer: The clear understanding of Allah's signs in nature and scripture.

questions.text: What is the 'Stable Earth' (Qarara)?
questions.answer: The earth as a firm and safe place for living, created by Allah.

questions.text: What are the 'Rivers' (Anhara) within the earth?
questions.answer: A source of water and life, mentioned as a sign of divine provision.

questions.text: What are the 'Firm Mountains' (Rawasiya)?
questions.answer: Anchors for the earth's crust, showing Allah's perfect engineering.

questions.text: What is the 'Barrier' (Hajiza) between the two seas?
questions.answer: The physical laws that prevent fresh and salt water from mixing completely.

questions.text: What is the 'Succession' (Khulafa' al-Ard)?
questions.answer: Mankind's role as stewards and inheritors of the earth.

questions.text: What is the 'Good news' (Bushra) sent by the winds?
questions.answer: The rain that brings life to the land.

questions.text: What is the 'Proof' (Burhan) requested from the polytheists?
questions.answer: Logical evidence for the existence of other gods, which they can never provide.

questions.text: What is the 'Praise' (Al-Hamd) due to Allah?
questions.answer: The recognition of His perfection and blessings.

questions.text: What is the 'Recitation' (Tilawah) of the Quran?
questions.answer: Reading and reflecting upon the divine word.

questions.text: What is the 'Final realization'?
questions.answer: The point when all people will finally see and admit the truth, though for many it will be too late.
